message:
feat(fsfs): tune adaptive debounce timing, add idle gate for semantic/quality stages, and extract quality viability check

Significantly tightens search responsiveness by reducing debounce
windows across all three search stages (lexical, semantic, quality) and
introducing idle gate timers that prevent semantic and quality searches
from firing while the user is still actively typing. This creates a
two-phase trigger: the debounce window must expire AND the user must
have paused typing for a mode-specific idle period.

- Reduce base debounce constants: lexical 8→6ms, semantic 140→36ms,
  quality 480→160ms with proportionally tighter min/max clamps
- Add idle gate system with per-stage min/max bounds that scale with
  typing cadence (semantic 96-260ms, quality 220-900ms)
- Extract quality_stage_viable() method to centralize quality search
  eligibility checks (vector index dimension, embedder identity, etc.)
- Add latency-adaptive fast trim/slow bump constants replacing inline
  magic numbers for debounce adjustment
- Add fast-stage snippet length cap and budget multiplier constants
- Reduce search poll interval from 12→8ms for tighter result delivery
- Wire idle gate into semantic_ready() and quality_ready() predicates
- Force-reset last_query_edit_at on manual quality refresh to bypass
  idle gate when user explicitly requests refresh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
